sections.heading: Differentiated Thyroid Carcinomas (PTC, FTC, oncocytic)
sections.content: Differentiated thyroid carcinomas (PTC, FTC, oncocytic) are characterized by alterations in the RAS/RAF/MAPK signaling pathway. BRAF V600E is the most common driver mutation in PTC, occurring in about 45% of cases; it is associated with poorer prognosis when concurrent with TERT promoter mutations. RET/PTC rearrangements and RAS mutations are also frequent in PTC. Follicular and oncocytic carcinomas commonly harbor RAS mutations, PAX8/PPARγ rearrangements, and, in the case of oncocytic carcinoma, unique mitochondrial DNA variations and copy number alterations.

sections.heading: NIFTP
sections.content: NIFTP, formerly a subtype of PTC, is characterized by RAS, PAX8/PPARγ, and/or BRAF mutations (excluding BRAF V600E) [Discussion, MS-5, MS-9, MS-10, MS-16, MS-53].

sections.heading: Medullary Thyroid Carcinoma
sections.content: Medullary thyroid carcinoma is driven by mutations in the RET proto-oncogene. Germline RET PVs cause hereditary forms (MEN2A, MEN2B, familial MTC). Somatic RET mutations (e.g., in codons 11, 13, 16) are found in at least 25% of sporadic MTC tumors and are associated with poorer prognosis [MEDU-1, MEDU-3, Discussion, MS-30, MS-31].

sections.heading: Anaplastic Thyroid Carcinoma
sections.content: Anaplastic thyroid carcinoma is characterized by loss of differentiation and multiple genetic alterations, including TP53 mutations, TERT promoter mutations, and, in a subset, BRAF V600E mutations (~20-40%). Approximately 50% of ATCs arise from or are coexistent with a differentiated thyroid carcinoma [ANAP-1, THYR-B 3/5, Discussion, MS-37, MS-51].
